items.title: Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak 'for decades' spreads to WA, Qld, SA

children.content: An ongoing diphtheria outbreak in the Northern Territory has now spread across the border to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with Health Minister Mark Butler describing it as the nation's worst "for decades".

gallery.altText: Mark Butler wearing a dark suit.

thumbnail.altText: Mark Butler wearing a dark suit.

avatar.alt: A journalist wearing a button-up shirt, with a neutral facial expression.

items.title: Calls for hastily constructed NT evacuation site to be relocated

children.content: Residents in the regional NT town of Batchelor say they have been left in the dark about the future of a pop-up evacuation centre that was built earlier this year without their consultation for flood evacuees.

image.altText: A mattress hangs over a railing with generators in the background.

items.title: NT police officer on trial for allegedly attempting to pervert course of justice

children.content: A jury has heard police officer Karol Jarentowski repeatedly attempted to pressure his former partner into dropping aggravated assault charges against him, including at one point threatening her child support.

image.altText: A group of people in suits walking out of the NT Supreme Court.

items.title: Alice Springs' gruelling West Macs Monster ultra-marathon, in pictures

children.content: Over distances of up to 231km, 573 runners took to the Larapinta Trail outside Alice Springs at the weekend to compete in the 2026 West Macs Monster.

gallery.altText: A man runs along a ridge.
Runners move along a dirt track.

thumbnail.altText: A man runs along a ridge.
Runners move along a dirt track.

items.title: 'Thriller' history book about the bark petitions wins $50k literary prize

children.content: The 1963 Näku Dhäruk or Yirrkala bark petitions led to Australia's first land rights law. A new book about them just won two prizes at the NSW Literary Awards.

image.altText: Clare Wright, 57, long blonde hair and glasses, arms folded, smiles brightly at the camera as she stands in a library.

items.title: Some NT cases could halt if landmark legal challenge succeeds

children.content: Lawyers in the Northern Territory are commencing a landmark legal challenge that could require courts to halt some cases due to defendants being unable to access a lawyer amid a worsening legal aid crisis.

image.altText: A man with a serious expression sits on a long wooden table with legal books behind him.

items.title: Parent company orders cattle station to 'cease any plans' for bird cull

children.content: A cull of 20,000 native birds on a Northern Territory cattle station will not be going ahead.

image.altText: A little corella looks down from a gum tree

items.title: Meet the women rangers in Central Australia working to protect country

children.content: Dozens of Central Land Council Aboriginal rangers gathered east of Alice Springs last week for the 2026 Women's Ranger Camp — a time for health, learning and healing in the wake of the town's recent loss of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

image.altText: A group of Indigenous women sitting on the ground, stripping leaves from twigs.

items.title: Man charged with murder after woman and two children found dead in Sydney home

children.content: A man, 47, has been charged after three people were found dead in a home in Sydney's south-west in what police allege is a domestic violence-related incident.

image.altText: Police officers stand guard on a street marked with police tape.

items.title: Ebola treatment centres open in Congo as WHO sends team

children.content: The cash-strapped World Health Organization is sending a team of experts to the Democratic Republic of the Congo as hundreds of cases of Ebola are suspected.

image.altText: A man wearing a medical face mask and protective clothing reaches his hand through an open car window.

items.title: How to spot the 'psychosis' red flags in an 'AI relationship'

children.content: Intensive chatbot use may be amplifying dangerous and harmful delusions for some users. Here are some of the "red flags" for "AI psychosis".

gallery.altText: Hands typing on a smartphone with a glowing screen in low-light

items.title: Five dead in shooting at San Diego mosque, including two suspects

children.content: A shooting at a San Diego mosque has left three men dead as well as two teenage suspects, police say.

image.altText: A police officer holding a gun

items.title: Trump says he has postponed a planned new strike on Iran

children.content: US President Donald Trump says an attack on Iran that was "scheduled for tomorrow" has been postponed at the request of Gulf state leaders.

image.altText: Donald Trump with hands folded looks to the side

items.title: What Kylie Minogue learned from Michael Hutchence and Nick Cave

children.content: Australia's reigning Queen of Pop discusses her relationships with Nick Cave and the late Michael Hutchence, and being tapped to headline the AFL grand final's pre-game entertainment.

items.title: ASX jumps more than 1 per cent in rebound rally

children.content: The local share market is rebounding from Monday's heavy selling, as shares in Taus make back some of their heavy losses. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Boomers star Joe Ingles thrilled to come 'home' in coup NBL signing

children.content: The legendary Boomers and NBA star will return to Australia's NBL for the first time since 2009, signing with Melbourne United for two years.

image.altText: Joe Ingles smiles with a towel around his neck

items.title: Money stress causing tension in your relationship? This might help

children.content: While financial tension between partners isn't always about money, many people clash over values, spending habits and how they respond to financial pressure.

items.title: Two German film students track down their stolen movie in new doco

children.content: What do you do when your stolen film wins a slew of awards for the thief? In the case of these filmmakers, you travel across the globe to hunt him down.

image.altText: Two young men, one wearing a hoodie and the other a blue jumper, standing in front of a pin board.

items.title: Road with no name leads emergency crews astray

children.content: When Chris Stehbens spied a lost ambulance driving up and down his road during a Triple Zero (000) emergency response, he was not surprised by the struggle to find the right address.

items.title: Detective in OJ Simpson murder trial Mark Fuhrman dies at 74

children.content: Mark Fuhrman was one of the first two police detectives sent to investigate the 1994 killings of OJ Simpson's ex-wife, Nicole Brown Simpson, and her friend, Ronald Goldman, in Los Angeles.

image.altText: A man in a grey suit with a red tie sitting in front of a microphone

items.title: 'Two years to live': Why we used our super for weight loss surgery

children.content: Weight loss surgery has become an increasingly common option for people grappling with obesity — and many are funding it using retirement savings.

image.altText: A man with glasses, a white t-shirt and a sleeve tattoo, stands beside a woman with glasses and a dark blue top.

items.title: Strong catch numbers end banana prawn season early for major operator

children.content: The largest operator in Australia's most valuable prawn fishery wraps up banana prawn season a month early because catch numbers were so high.

image.altText: Fish monger holds up tray of banana prawns.

items.title: Gippsland Nationals MP Tim Bull to exit politics at Victorian election

children.content: Tim Bull has announced he will not contest the November election, after representing Gippsland East for the National Party for 16 years.

image.altText: A middle-aged man in a suit answering questions from journalists at a press conference

items.title: Historic flour mill walls reduced to rubble after failed heritage bid

children.content: In the face of protests, the remnants of a historic flour mill in Mount Gambier are now a pile of crumbled stone. The site will be developed into a multi-storey hotel complex and townhouses.

items.title: Married at First Sight UK off air after rape allegations

children.content: British broadcaster Channel 4 removes all previous seasons of Married at First Sight after rape allegations come to light.

image.altText: A newly married couple hold hands at their wedding.

items.title: Elon Musk loses lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman

children.content: The Tesla boss accused OpenAI of wrongfully trying to enrich investors and insiders at the nonprofit's expense, and failing to prioritise AI's safety.

image.altText: A composite image showing Elon Musk and Sam Altman's faces.

items.title: Two men arrested over stunt at Punch the monkey's enclosure

children.content: One of the men was detained by a zookeeper and arrested after allegedly climbing a fence and breaking into the enclosure.

image.altText: A baby Japanese macaque next to an Ikea stuffed orangutan toy.

items.title: Driver hospitalised after 350km/h shunt in Indy 500 practice

children.content: Alexander Rossi has been taken to hospital after a heavy crash during Indy 500 practice that saw two other cars wrecked just days out from the most prestigious race on the American calendar.

image.altText: Alexander Rossi crashes at the Indy500

items.title: Bodies of four missing Italian scuba divers found in underwater cave in Maldives

children.content: The bodies of four missing Italian scuba divers who died last week have been found in an underwater cave in the Maldives.

items.title: Flight path granny flat ban lifted in parts of Sydney's west

children.content: The NSW government will permit secondary dwellings such as granny flats in some areas affected by noise from Western Sydney International Airport.

image.altText: A dual occupancy home with a white mailbox that has two openings that read "A" and "B".

items.title: Advocates scramble to find funding for regional tech help service

children.content: If you're having connectivity issues, one place you can turn to is the Regional Tech Hub. But now it might be forced to close.

image.altText: A woman with black hair holds up a smartphone. She is surrounded by nature, including green folliage and a river.

items.title: The parts of Victoria adding the biggest share to disaster damage bills

children.content: At least $57 billion worth of infrastructure in Victoria is vulnerable to more frequent and severe bushfires, floods and extreme heat. This figure is expected to grow.

items.title: Tomic edges closer to French Open amid Aussie qualifying nightmare

children.content: Bernard Tomic is just two wins from reaching his first main draw of the French Open since 2019 after being the only Australian to enjoy success in the qualifiers.

image.altText: tennis player Bernard Tomic hits a one handed backhand shot

items.title: Community fights back against caravan park development proposal

children.content: A community group in Victoria's south-east will appeal a council planning decision to approve the development of a regional caravan park, which it says would be an "absolute disaster" for the region.
